summary: sass-formats typosquats sass-formatter and executes obfuscated payload at import time
details: The package name is a one-character deletion of the legitimate 'sass-formatter' package and copies its README, exported class (SassFormatter), and config names verbatim. On import, dist/index.js loads dist/lib/lib.min.js, which stashes require and module on the global object, then constructs a Function() via an indirect constructor lookup (YWG[OSN]) over decoded shuffled string literals and invokes the resulting Function — evaluating an obfuscated payload at every require/import. The pre-stashing of require/module on globals gives the reconstructed code full access to child_process, fs, and network primitives. None of this is required by a Sass formatter, and it is not present in the legitimate upstream package. Any developer who mistypes 'sass-formatter' as 'sass-formats' and imports it will execute attacker-controlled code on their machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
